preview_text:
The model favors England to win at 63%, while Panama's chances stand at 12%. England's win probability substantially exceeds that of the underdog, though the model assigns Panama a non-trivial chance rather than treating the match as a foregone conclusion.

The 24.8% draw probability reflects a gap between the favorites and underdog that permits a competitive contest. A draw ranks as the second-most likely outcome, suggesting the match could be closely contested in regular time, even if England enters as the statistical favorite. The three-outcome spread indicates neither a heavily skewed matchup nor an even pairing.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books price England's win probability at 74%, a gap of roughly 11 percentage points above the model's estimate. Simultaneously, books assign draws only 16%, compared to the model's 24.8%. Polymarket prices align more closely with the sportsbook consensus than with the model. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate; the discrepancy merits attention from users forming their own views.
